description_long: This shrub consists of a dense cluster of slender, upright green stems topped with delicate, pale inflorescences resembling grass or meadow plants. The stems are thin and flexible, with a natural, slightly arching growth habit that gives the shrub a soft, airy appearance. The foliage is sparse, with most of the visual mass coming from the long, narrow leaves and the feathery, light-colored flower heads. The overall shape is rounded at the base, fanning outward and upward, creating a gentle, bushy silhouette. The coloration is predominantly light green with hints of pale white or cream in the flowering parts. This type of shrub is commonly found in urban parks, roadside plantings, or landscaped medians.
description: A bushy, airy shrub with slender green stems and pale, feathery flower heads, forming a soft, rounded cluster. The plant has a natural, meadow-like appearance with sparse foliage and delicate inflorescences.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full, rounded cluster of upright green stems and pale flower heads, highlighting the shrub's bushy and airy structure.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thinness of the shrub, with stems and flower heads arching outward, emphasizing its sparse, vertical growth.
description_view_2: Rear view is similar to the front, displaying the dense arrangement of stems and inflorescences, but with slight variation in the spread of the foliage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the slender profile and arching stems, with the delicate flower heads visible along the top.
